final_answer:
Implemented: the production signing fix is in, and real-node `tx stake approve-validator` no longer fails with `invalid pubkey`.

Why it matters: stake/validator approval now signs with the `--from` keyring account (correct signer), so real Gold Chain validator txs can reach actual stake logic instead of failing at signature verification.

Changed (surgical, single file):
- Switched `BroadcastMsg` from helper priv-validator signing to keyring `--from` signing path in [tx.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/common/cli/tx.go#L52).
- Added local signer flow that uses `tx.Sign(..., clientCtx.FromName, ...)` and returns `TxResponse` for strict code checks in [tx.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/common/cli/tx.go#L68).
- Removed panic risk on confirm-path mismatch; now returns explicit error in [tx.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/common/cli/tx.go#L101).

Validation run:
- `make build` (passed).
- `go test ./common/cli ./x/stake/client/cli ./x/checkpoint/client/cli ./x/gilt/client/cli ./x/stake/...` (passed).
- `go test ./bridge/broadcaster` (passed).
- Real 4-node local run on this server:
  - `tx stake approve-validator ... --from val0 ...` returned success with tx hash `0777C0547D37F757B83681156E4956FF3A121F21650753761FFBF2C3BC85E7E7`.
  - `invalid_pubkey_present=no`.

What should be done next:
1. If you want, I can now commit only this file change.
2. If you want stricter coverage, I can add a focused regression test for `common/cli` ensuring missing `FromName`/keyring fails early.
